$comment: Structured, dated AI model-pricing data for scripts/fleet/estimate-ai-cost.mts and the routing heuristic. GENERIC + PUBLIC ONLY: per-service vendor list prices (USD per million tokens) and the generic plan STRUCTURE (kind / constraint / public list price) — never an org's private budgets, usage, or subscription specifics, which live solely in private runtime config, never in git. Each service carries its own `snapshot` (capture date) + `pricingSource` (the vendor page) so staleness is per-provider and honest. Refreshed by the updating-pricing sub-skill (weekly via /updating, or on demand via /update-pricing): it reads the vendor page (or, when a number isn't directly available, mines the researching-recency feed via scripts/fleet/source-pricing-feed.mts) then hands sourced prices to scripts/fleet/update-model-pricing.mts, which owns the write so the shape + sort order + snapshot date stay canonical. Do not hand-edit numbers — re-source so the snapshot stays honest. Edit in socket-wheelhouse/template/base/ then cascade.

notes: multipliers.batch 0.5 = the Batch API's 50%-off on input + output.
multipliers.cacheRead 0.1 = a prompt-cache hit costs 0.1x base input.
multipliers.cacheWrite5m 1.25 / cacheWrite1h 2.0 are write-time input multipliers.
Opus 4.7 and later use a new tokenizer that can emit up to ~35% more tokens for the same text — fold this into token-profile estimates for Opus/Fable, not into the per-token price.
Fast mode (Opus only, research preview) bills at a premium and is not used by the fleet — omitted here.
suspended:true is DATA, not a code branch: the router reads it to skip a model (Fable 5 + Mythos 5 suspended 2026-06-12, 'use Opus 4.8'); it self-heals when the flag clears.
Metered per-token list prices from the serverless pricing page; re-source per snapshot. No org budget here — that is private runtime config.
Flat-rate plan: models bill under the plan (billing:'plan'), not per token. The generic plan STRUCTURE (price + public rate limits) is here; private usage is not.
